critic_raw:
ASSESSOR 1:
Verdict: empirical_test_agreed

The vendor baseline was not optimally configured (generic mode, English-only) while the startup's model was purpose-trained (receipt-fine-tuned, bilingual). This creates two compounding configuration disadvantages for the vendor. Whether the startup's model is genuinely better requires controlling for both. An empirical test with the reconfigured vendor is the appropriate resolution.
defender_raw:
ASSESSOR 2:
Verdict: empirical_test_agreed

Configuration gaps are well-identified. The startup's specialization and bilingual training are real capabilities. The gap may persist or narrow under fair conditions. Empirical test is the right resolution.

ASSESSOR 3:
Verdict: empirical_test_agreed

Two specific, testable issues. Both are correctable. The empirical test is well-defined. The startup may well maintain an advantage, but the magnitude of the current 8.4 pp gap is uncertain under fair conditions.
adjudication_raw: Unanimous empirical_test_agreed across all three assessors. Issues are genuine, direction of advantage is plausible, magnitude is empirically uncertain. ETD: run vendor with receipt-specific parser and bilingual settings; measure accuracy gap on same evaluation set.

empirical_test.success_criterion: Startup model accuracy gap ≥ 5 pp over vendor using receipt-specific enterprise parser and bilingual language configuration
empirical_test.failure_criterion: Gap below 5 pp or vendor leads under fair configuration
